markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Define Handshake-native transcript/OCR import path for PDFs without making OCR an undeclared external runtime dependency. This MT belongs to SourceIngestionAndEvidence. Ingest project, governance, media, and research sources with evidence and failure receipts.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Source ingestion must be allowlisted, hash-based, restartable, and secret-aware.
PDF/media extraction failures must produce receipts and repairable metadata rather than silently indexing partial content.
scope.expected_behavior: PdfTranscriptImportPath leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: PdfTranscriptImportPath is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 0903cdcf; real-PG tests green (group total 80 passed/0 failed; lopdf 0.41 MIT only dep added). Pending adversarial code review before READY_FOR_VALIDATION.

concerns: No-OCR contract honored: extract_pdf_text never runs OCR; an image-only PDF returns a typed NO_TEXT_LAYER error (never empty-success) carrying the report detail with OCR_NEEDED guidance. OCR is an out-of-band operator step that re-imports a transcript ARTIFACT (media_transcript path), not an undeclared runtime dependency — matches MT-087 contract and OPERATOR LAW (no ASR/OCR daemons).

Partial extraction (some pages text, some image) -> Ok with failed_pages populated and the caller writes a 'partial' receipt + repair entry (OCR the failed pages). Whitespace-only extracted page is demoted to a failed_page (never a silent empty span).

INHERITS the MT-086 catch_unwind gap: extract_pdf_text calls load_document twice and doc.extract_text per page with no panic guard; a crafted PDF panicking inside lopdf aborts the pass. Same hardening recommendation as MT-086.
evasion_probes: image-only -> NO_TEXT_LAYER typed error, no spans CONFIRMED (pipeline mt086_image_only + fixture mt096 scanned.pdf).
mixed -> Partial, spans for readable pages, failed_pages names page 2 with OCR_NEEDED, repair enqueued CONFIRMED (pipeline mt087_mixed_pdf).
every text-bearing page failing extraction -> typed NO_TEXT_LAYER (not empty success) CONFIRMED.

extracted text is trimmed; secret preflight runs over the CONCATENATED extracted page text and BLOCKS on HIGH severity before any page span is stored (engine.rs:1152-1170) CONFIRMED — a secret embedded in a PDF text layer is caught.
PDF spans go through redact_spans (secret_scan=true) for MEDIUM redaction CONFIRMED.
evidence_checked: engine.rs:1139-1201 PDF branch (block on HIGH over extracted text; partial/NoTextLayer)

hardening_closed.note: Adversarial finding fixed + real-PG negative test green (8 hardening tests + 70 unit, no regression).

evidence: Hardened 5876feaf: ingestion secret-leak/lopdf/dead-letter fixes verified; boundary-seam fixture shows ZERO raw secret bytes in spans; DB redaction guard (0210) + receipt NOT NULL (0211) enforced.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-087 requires a Handshake-native PDF text/transcript import path that never makes OCR an undeclared runtime dependency, emits typed failures plus repairable metadata instead of silent partial indexing, and persists spans/receipts/repair to real PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority anchored to spec 2.3.13.11. The code (pdf.rs extract_pdf_text, spans.rs SpanAnchor::PdfPage, migration 0163, engine.rs PDF branch) implements exactly this and is proven against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL with no SQLite/mock fallback.
findings: pdf.rs:348-420 extract_pdf_text: text-layer pages -> PdfPageText spans; image-only PDF -> typed IngestionErrorClass::NoTextLayer with OCR_NEEDED guidance in detail (NEVER empty success); mixed PDF -> Ok with explicit failed_pages. Caller must write partial receipt. Matches MT-087 no-silent-partial-indexing requirement.

No-OCR contract honored: pdf.rs:14-19 + 342-345 document that Handshake runs no OCR; scanned-document import path is an operator-provided transcript artifact routed through MediaTranscript (engine.rs:1124). OCR is never an undeclared external runtime dependency, satisfying the MT scope statement.

pdf.rs:129-150 guard_lopdf wraps load_document/extract via catch_unwind, converting crafted-PDF panics to typed IngestionErrorClass::Internal so one poison file degrades to one failed file rather than aborting the pass (MT-086/087 #5). lopdf 0.41 is pure-Rust MIT, no daemon/native dep — within the bundled-library constraint; no SQLite/Docker/external engine introduced.

spans.rs:31-34 SpanAnchor::PdfPage{page} and migration 0163_knowledge_ingestion_spans.sql:29-34 anchor_kind CHECK including 'pdf_page' match spec 2.3.13.11 KnowledgeSpan anchor kinds (.GOV/spec/master-spec-v02.194/spec-modules/02-system-architecture.md:2694). Table is FK-bound to receipts, carries content_hash verifiability + EventLedger evidence, registered as authority_class='authority' (0163:68-73).

engine.rs:1172-1227 PDF branch wires extract_pdf_text into the pipeline: spans persisted via store.replace_source_spans (engine.rs:445-453), repair enqueued via store.enqueue_repair (engine.rs:500-513), and MT-091 secret preflight (scan_text over concatenated extracted page text) blocks on must_block() before any span is stored.

Real-PG proof: tests/knowledge_ingestion_pipeline_tests.rs:204-331 (mt086_mt087 text-layer -> 2 pdf_page spans, no repair; mt086 image-only -> Failed receipt + NO_TEXT_LAYER + queued repair with reason_class NO_TEXT_LAYER; mt087 mixed -> Partial receipt, spans_failed=1, failed_pages[0].page==2, repair enqueued). Harness knowledge_ingestion_support.rs/knowledge_pg_support.rs connect a real PostgresDatabase to an isolated schema with the full migration chain (POSTGRES_TEST_URL/DATABASE_URL), explicitly 'no SQLite, no mocks'; FileIngestOutcome.spans are StoredSpan rows from store.replace_source_spans.
Grep of pdf.rs + spans.rs for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow-on'/'pending MT-'/Live*Unavailable returned no hits on the required-behavior path.
Non-blocking aside (paperwork only): MT contract cites v02.192 spec anchors; section 2.3.13.11 exists unchanged in current v02.194. Per ground rules this is not a fail reason.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
